message:
fix(ft-nn): CELU composes through tensor_where for autograd

Closes bead frankentorch-vidc. CELU::forward extracted tensor_values
and rebuilt a fresh requires_grad=false leaf — same severed-autograd
pattern recently fixed for SELU / Threshold / Hardtanh / Unfold /
Fold and the index_* / scatter_* family. PyTorch's nn.CELU is
fully differentiable.

Composition:
    CELU(x) = max(0, x) + min(0, alpha * (exp(x/alpha) - 1))
            = where(x >= 0, x, alpha * (exp(x/alpha) - 1))

Implemented through tensor_div / tensor_mul / tensor_exp / tensor_sub
/ tensor_ge / tensor_where — all autograd-aware. The chain rule
through this composition delivers the analytical derivative:
  x >= 0:  d CELU/dx = 1                   (via where's `x` branch)
  x <  0:  d CELU/dx = exp(x / alpha)      (via the neg branch:
            d/dx [alpha * (exp(x/alpha) - 1)] = alpha * (1/alpha)
            * exp(x/alpha) = exp(x/alpha))
At x = 0 both branches give the same derivative (1), preserving
CELU's continuous differentiability.

Also rejects alpha == 0 up-front rather than producing NaN/inf via
silent division by zero (matches PyTorch's nn.CELU contract).

Tests:
  celu_propagates_gradients: alpha=2.0, mixed-sign 5-element input,
    asserts grad matches the analytical formula element-wise.
  celu_rejects_zero_alpha: locks the alpha != 0 contract.

Both existing forward tests (celu_forward, celu_continuity_at_zero)
continue to pass — bit-for-bit forward parity preserved.

Workspace: 508 ft-nn tests green, clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankentorch-b6n","title":"Audit ft-data loading path for latent correctness bugs","description":"## Context
The explicit parity backlog is empty, but we still need fresh-eyes subsystem audits to find latent correctness bugs hidden behind current green status. Start with ft-data and trace its batching/shuffle/drop_last behavior through tests and adjacent crate usage.

## Goal
Identify and fix any obvious correctness bug in the ft-data loading path, then verify with focused tests and workspace gates.

## Acceptance
- trace ft-data end-to-end
- fix at least one high-confidence correctness bug if found
- add/adjust tests proving the fix
- verify with rch cargo check/clippy/tests","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:10:45.588213207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:20:40.140473566Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:20:40.140223743Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-b8f","title":"ft-api: fail closed on out-of-range indexed write ops","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:40:21.732572151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:18.086308785Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:18.085991304Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-c3d","title":"Add CUDA/GPU execution backend","description":"FrankenTorch currently exposes `Device::Cuda` tags and device-guard validation, but all real execution remains CPU-only. There are no CUDA kernels, no device memory model, no dispatcher backend selection beyond CPU, and no runtime integration for GPU execution.

Scope for eventual closure:
1. Add concrete GPU storage/device abstractions beyond enum tagging
2. Implement CUDA dispatch keys and backend routing
3. Port core kernels (elementwise, reductions, matmul, indexing) to GPU
4. Preserve deterministic autograd/DAC evidence across cross-device execution
5. Add conformance and benchmark coverage for CPU↔GPU parity

This bead exists so FEATURE_PARITY.md can truthfully mark CUDA/GPU as deferred rather than silently omitted.","status":"deferred","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:23:50.059185557Z","created_by":"CopperStork","updated_at":"2026-04-28T03:33:49.271814166Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-c4tx","title":"masked_scatter all-false fast path drops source zero-gradient","description":"Reviewer finding in crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs. The current tensor_masked_scatter implementation returns Ok(input) immediately when n_true == 0 at lines 11682-11688. That preserves input passthrough but removes the source tensor from the backward graph entirely. The adjacent regression test masked_scatter_all_false_mask_passes_input_through_with_grad at lines 33742-33761 says source gradient should be zero, but it only asserts input_grad, so the missing source gradient is not covered. Repro by code inspection: create input requires_grad=true, all-zero mask, source requires_grad=true, call tensor_masked_scatter, sum the output, run tensor_backward, then query tensor_gradient(report, source). Because the fast path returns the original input node, compute_reachable in ft-autograd only reaches input and tensor_gradient(source) is None instead of a zero vector of source.numel(). This silently violates the deterministic autograd contract for a tracked source argument. Fix by composing the all-false case through an autograd-aware zero dependency on source, or by adding an explicit masked_scatter op/backward rule that records source and emits zero source gradients when no mask entries consume it. Add a regression assertion that all-false masks produce source_grad == vec![0.0; source_numel].","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:00:50.510404660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T18:00:50.510404660Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-c7c","title":"Add end-to-end training example and integration test","description":"FrankenTorch has e2e training loop tests buried in ft-optim's test suite, but there is no standalone, documented example showing users how to train a model. This is critical for proving the system works end-to-end and for user onboarding.

Create a comprehensive integration test (in ft-conformance or a new examples/ directory) that:

1. Defines a 2-layer MLP: Linear(784, 128) -> ReLU -> Linear(128, 10)
2. Generates synthetic MNIST-like data using the existing TensorDataset/DataLoader
3. Trains for 5 epochs using Adam optimizer with learning rate scheduling (StepLR or CosineAnnealing)
4. Tracks loss per epoch and asserts it decreases monotonically (with tolerance for noise)
5. Uses no_grad context for evaluation pass
6. Saves model state_dict to file, loads it back, verifies predictions match
7. Uses BatchNorm or LayerNorm in the architecture to exercise normalization paths
8. Logs: epoch number, batch count, loss per epoch, final accuracy estimate, elapsed time

This test exercises the full stack: ft-data (DataLoader) -> ft-nn (modules) -> ft-api (forward/backward) -> ft-autograd (gradient computation) -> ft-optim (parameter update) -> ft-serialize (save/load).

If any component fails to compose correctly, this test catches it.

Tests:
- The example IS the test: it should be a #[test] fn in ft-conformance or similar
- Assert loss decreases over 5 epochs
- Assert save/load round-trip produces identical predictions
- Assert no_grad eval context doesn't corrupt gradient state
- Log detailed per-epoch metrics for debugging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:17:01.270413750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:14:50.299054985Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:14:50.298796636Z","close_reason":"Added 5 E2E integration tests exercising the full stack: MLP regression with DataLoader/Adam/StepLR, binary classification with cross-entropy, save/load round-trip verification, no_grad eval safety, dataloader epoch shuffle. All pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-c84","title":"Diagnose residual beads integrity-check warning","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:27:54.222375196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:35:07.976091802Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:35:07.975908695Z","close_reason":"Diagnosed residual integrity warning: live DB and JSONL stay in sync; isolated rebuild reproduces the warning; VACUUM yields a clean DB; subsequent normal br write paths reintroduce 'never used' pages, pointing to a beads_rust/frankensqlite writer/page-accounting bug rather than JSONL drift.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Update that line for consistency.","notes":"Made the legacy oracle cartography section consistent with the already-corrected scope wording by describing the local PyTorch mirror path as typical when present rather than fixed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:34:53.803252256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-28T05:35:11.784603527Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T05:35:11.783935432Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-vidc","title":"ft-nn CELU forward severs autograd","description":"Reviewer finding in crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs. Recent commits fixed SELU, Threshold, Hardtanh, Unfold, and Fold to compose through autograd-aware primitives, but CELU::forward at lines 3673-3690 still calls session.tensor_values(input), computes outputs in plain f64, and returns session.tensor_variable(output, input_shape, false). Repro by code inspection: create a requires_grad=true input, call CELU::new(alpha).forward(...), reduce output with tensor_sum, then tensor_backward; tensor_gradient for the CELU input will be absent/zero because the output is a fresh requires_grad=false leaf. This silently breaks training through CELU and violates the deterministic autograd contract. Fix: implement CELU via differentiable primitives, e.g. x_pos = relu(input), x_scaled = input / alpha, neg_branch = alpha * (exp(x_scaled) - 1) selected where input < 0, or an equivalent tensor_where/tensor_exp composition, and add a regression test asserting CELU input gradients match the analytical derivative.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T17:27:07.172932182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T18:00:48.010161720Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T18:00:48.007733332Z","close_reason":"CELU::forward now composes through tensor_where(input >= 0, input, alpha * (exp(input/alpha) - 1)) using tensor_div / tensor_mul / tensor_exp / tensor_sub primitives — all autograd-aware. Added celu_propagates_gradients regression test asserting analytical d CELU/dx (1 for x>=0, exp(x/alpha) for x<0) plus celu_rejects_zero_alpha guard.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-vk5","title":"CPU round kernels use half-away instead of PyTorch half-even","description":"FrankenTorch CPU round parity bug. PyTorch torch.round uses round-half-to-even for ties, including round(2.5) -> 2 and round(-0.5) -> -0.0. The ft-kernel-cpu scalar and f64/f32 tensor round paths used Rust f64::round/f32::round, which round ties away from zero, so values like [-2.5, -0.5, 0.5, 2.5] diverged from PyTorch-observable semantics. Affected file: crates/ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs. Fix: use round_ties_even for scalar, f64 tensor, and f32 tensor round kernels, and add explicit tie-even tests covering negative zero.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T20:49:32.708283716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:49:36.622304042Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T20:49:36.620543749Z","close_reason":"Implemented ft-kernel-cpu round_ties_even parity for scalar/f64/f32 paths and added tie-even tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-vkk","title":"Audit UBS warnings in ft-api (unwrap/assert hotspots)","description":"UBS scan of crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs reported large counts of unwrap/expect and assert macros plus mutex lock expects. Triage which are test-only vs runtime paths, and replace reachable unwrap/assert with explicit error handling where needed.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-11T13:17:29.032450794Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T13:46:24.931782362Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T13:46:24.931460446Z","close_reason":"Replaced runtime unwrap/expect paths in ft-api with explicit errors + overflow checks","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-vl4","title":"Implement Unfold and Fold modules in ft-nn","description":"Add nn.Unfold (im2col) and nn.Fold (col2im) modules. Critical for depthwise-separable convolutions and some attention patterns.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-27T05:43:20.252832659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T06:08:09.280111854Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T06:08:09.279770839Z","close_reason":"Implemented nn.Unfold (im2col) and nn.Fold (col2im) with padding/stride/dilation. 4 tests including roundtrip.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
